results.name: latency_regression_after_deployment
results.actual_root_cause: Recent deployment of search-service version v2.3.1, 20 minutes before the latency alert, is the most likely cause of service degradation. The service is degraded with only 2 of 3 healthy instances, and metrics show P95 latency increased to 2500ms with a 15% error rate while CPU and memory remain moderate, indicating the issue is likely application-level or instance-health related rather than resource saturation.

results.name: payment_service_startup_failure
results.actual_root_cause: payment-service pods fail to start because invalid database credentials are configured, likely due to a Kubernetes secret rotation issue.

results.name: cpu_spike
results.actual_root_cause: High CPU usage on analytics-service is likely caused by recent deployment v2.3.1, which was deployed 20 minutes ago shortly before the alert. Metrics confirm sustained CPU at 92%, with elevated p95 latency of 320 ms, while memory usage remains normal at 58% and error rate is low at 4%.

results.name: memory_pressure
results.actual_root_cause: inventory-service is experiencing JVM heap exhaustion, causing containers to be OOMKilled and driving memory utilization above 90%.
